meta.name: Lamb Shawarma
meta.subtitle: Garlic-lemon, warm-spice lamb with shawarma char—sheet-pan, skillet, or grill options

source.name: SSA Canonical Catalogs
notes: Home-kitchen friendly (no vertical spit required).
Designed for strong shawarma flavor with lamb-forward richness.

summary: Lamb shawarma with classic warm spices, lemon, and garlic—cooked hot for browned edges and served in wraps, bowls, or plates with tahini or garlic sauce.

yield.notes: About 2.5–3 lb lamb yields ~6 hearty wraps/bowls (or 8 smaller servings).

heat.note: Optional cayenne or Aleppo pepper for heat.

optional.label: Grill
optional.notes: Excellent for lamb—adds smoke and quick browning.

optional.label: Air fryer
optional.notes: Great for small batches (single layer; shake halfway).

optional.label: Food processor
optional.notes: Optional for turning onion/garlic into a paste quickly.

notes: No dairy required in the base recipe.
If serving with tahini sauce, note sesame as an allergen.

ingredients.label: Lamb (shoulder, leg, or sirloin), thinly sliced or cut into thin strips
notes: Shoulder is most forgiving and flavorful.
Leg is leaner—avoid overcooking.
Freeze 20–30 minutes to firm up for thin slicing; slice across the grain.

ingredients.label: Yellow onion, grated or very finely minced
Ground sumac
ingredients.notes: Very shawarma-shop style brightness; optional but recommended.

ingredients.label: White vinegar or apple cider vinegar

steps.title: Slice the lamb thinly
instructions: Freeze lamb 20–30 minutes to firm it up (optional but helpful).
Slice very thinly across the grain (about 1/8-inch) or cut into thin strips.
Aim for uniform thickness so everything browns at the same rate.
checks: Sliced across the grain for tenderness.

steps.title: Mix marinade
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, and grated onion.
Add salt, pepper, cumin, coriander, paprika, cinnamon, and allspice.
Add optional cardamom, clove, sumac, Aleppo pepper, and vinegar if using. Mix well.
checks: Spices are fully blended; marinade smells bright and warm-spiced.

steps.title: Marinate the lamb
instructions: Add lamb to the marinade and toss thoroughly to coat.
Cover and refrigerate at least 45 minutes (ideal: 2–8 hours).
If using lean lamb leg, keep marinate closer to 2–6 hours for best texture.
checks: Lamb is evenly coated; spices cling to the surface.

steps.title: Cook (sheet-pan, oven method)
instructions: Heat oven to 450°F. Place a rack in the upper third of the oven.
Line a sheet pan (or use a rack on the pan if you want extra airflow).
Spread lamb in a single layer (use 2 pans if needed).
Roast 8–12 minutes until browned at edges. Broil 1–2 minutes at the end for extra char (watch closely).
Rest 3 minutes before serving.

temperature.notes: High heat for browned edges; avoid steaming by not overcrowding.

checks: Edges are browned; lamb is still juicy.
If liquids pool heavily, you overcrowded—use two pans next time.

steps.title: Cook (fast skillet method)
instructions: Heat a cast-iron or heavy skillet over high heat until very hot.
Cook lamb in batches in a single layer, 60–90 seconds per side, until browned.
Transfer to a plate; repeat. Combine and rest 2–3 minutes before serving.
checks: Lamb is seared quickly (browned), not simmered.

steps.title: Serve
instructions: Wrap: Spread toum or tahini sauce on warm pita/flatbread, add lamb, pickled turnips, tomatoes, cucumbers, and parsley.
Bowl: Serve over rice, fries, or chopped salad; drizzle sauce and add pickles.
Plate: Serve with roasted vegetables and a lemony salad for balance.
checks: Taste and adjust: add lemon, sumac, or pickles if it needs more brightness.

refrigerate.notes: Reheat in a hot skillet to re-crisp edges; microwave softens texture.

freeze.notes: Freeze cooked lamb flat in bags. Reheat thawed in a skillet or hot oven.

weeknightTips: Marinate in the morning; sheet-pan roast at dinner.
For fastest dinner: slice thin + 45 min marinade + skillet sear.
scalingNotes: Double batch needs two pans or multiple skillet batches to avoid steaming.

notes: Ingredient IDs are SSA-style; map via ingredient alias catalogs if your IDs differ.
Sumac/Aleppo are optional but help the 'shawarma shop' flavor.
Lamb loves brightness—sumac, lemon, and pickles help balance richness.
If your lamb is very lean, shorten cook time and skip long broiling.
For extra aroma, add 1 tsp ground ginger (optional) to the spice blend.
Thread strips onto skewers or use larger thin slices.
Grill over high heat just to brown and cook through, then slice and serve.
Rest 3 minutes before serving.
Air fry at 400°F for 6–10 minutes, shaking halfway, until browned edges appear.
Cook in a single layer; do multiple batches if needed.
Use the optional sumac and add 1 extra tbsp lemon juice.
Finish cooked lamb with a squeeze of lemon and a pinch of sumac.
Add 1 tsp cayenne or 2 tsp Aleppo pepper.
Serve with a spicy garlic sauce or hot sauce.
Marinate lamb in the refrigerator.
Avoid overcrowding pans to ensure safe, even cooking and good browning.
Discard marinade that contacted raw lamb unless boiled.
